question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario to cause a permanent cache inconsistency where contractSlotsCache holds a slot key but contractStateCache has expired the corresponding value, so that findStorageBatch() is called, the DB query fails due to partition, and the slot key remains in contractSlotsCache forever pointing to a value that can never be refreshed until the per-contract cache expires?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a resource exhaustion attack by exploiting the fact that each unique contractId gets its own Caffeine cache instance via cacheManagerSlotsPerContract.getCache(contractId.toString()), and during a network partition the attacker sends requests for millions of unique contractIds, causing the JVM to allocate millions of cache objects that are never cleaned up because the contractSlotsCache TTL has not expired?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a stale read attack on DeFi contracts by pre-caching a contract's storage slot value (e.g., a price oracle slot) just before a partition, then during the partition submitting eth_call simulations that read the stale cached price, obtaining transaction simulations based on incorrect state that would not reflect actual on-chain conditions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() uses contractStateRepository.findStorageBatch() with a List<byte[]> parameter, and during a network partition with a partial DB connection, the IN-clause query may return a partial result set without throwing an exception, causing some slots to be cached with correct values and others to remain as EMPTY_VALUE, creating a permanently inconsistent cache state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageByBlockTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a complete outage of the eth_call historical simulation feature by sending a high volume of findStorageByBlockTimestamp() requests during partition, each of which fails with a DB exception that propagates up through ContractStorageReadableKVState.readFromDataSource() and crashes the EVM execution context, causing all historical simulations to return 500 errors?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a cache stampede by waiting for the contractStateCache TTL to expire during a partition, then sending a burst of concurrent requests for the same slot key, causing all threads to simultaneously miss the cache, all call findStorageBatch(), all attempt the DB query against the partitioned DB, and all fail simultaneously, amplifying the load on the DB reconnection attempt?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the contractStateCache returns a non-null, non-EMPTY_VALUE cached byte array for a slot that has been updated on-chain during the partition, causing the EVM to execute contract logic with the pre-partition storage value and produce simulation results that are inconsistent with the actual on-chain state, misleading users about transaction outcomes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the generateCacheKey() method which uses new SimpleKey(contractId, slotKey) where slotKey is a byte array, and SimpleKey uses Arrays.deepHashCode for array parameters, to craft two different byte arrays that produce the same SimpleKey hash, causing a cache collision that serves wrong slot values during a network partition when the collision cannot be resolved by a fresh DB query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a split-brain state between the contractSlotsCache (which tracks which slots have been requested) and the contractStateCache (which tracks slot values), where the contractStateCache is cleared or expires during partition but contractSlotsCache retains all slot keys, causing findStorageBatch() to attempt to re-fetch all historical slot keys from the partitioned DB in a single massive query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the contractSlotsCache stores ByteBuffer objects as keys, and ByteBuffer equality is based on remaining bytes (position to limit), so that two ByteBuffer objects wrapping the same byte array but with different positions could be stored as separate entries in the cache, causing duplicate slot keys to be sent in the batch query and potentially causing DB-level issues during a network partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a long-term data integrity issue by observing that the contractStateCache has no write-through or write-behind mechanism, so values cached before the partition remain valid in cache even after the partition heals and the DB has been updated, causing the service to serve stale data for the entire duration of the cache TTL after partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause an integer overflow or memory issue in the ArrayList construction new ArrayList<byte[]>(cachedSlotKeys.size()), if the cachedSlotKeys.size() returns an unexpectedly large value due to concurrent insertions during the partition, causing the ArrayList to be allocated with an incorrect initial capacity and potentially throwing an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageByBlockTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a timing-based information disclosure by measuring the response time of findStorageByBlockTimestamp() calls, where cached-path requests (findStorage) return quickly from cache while uncached historical requests fail slowly with DB timeout, allowing the attacker to fingerprint which slots are cached and infer recent access patterns?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a denial-of-service by sending requests that trigger findStorageBatch() with a contractId that has thousands of cached slot keys, causing the batch query to construct a SQL IN-clause with thousands of parameters that exceeds the DB's maximum parameter limit, causing a DB error even after partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a cache pollution attack by sending requests for arbitrary slot keys of a victim contract before the partition, causing those keys to be permanently tracked in contractSlotsCache, so that after partition recovery every legitimate request for that contract triggers a batch query that includes all the attacker's bogus slot keys, increasing DB load and query latency indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractStateRepository.findStorageBatch() returns a List<ContractSlotValue> without any guarantee of ordering or completeness during a partial network partition, causing the loop that populates contractStateCache to store incorrect slot-value associations if the DB returns rows in a different order than expected, leading to wrong storage values being served to EVM executions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a permanent denial-of-service for a specific contract's storage reads by flooding the contractSlotsCache with the maximum allowed number of slot keys before the partition, so that during partition all requests for that contract fail (batch query fails), and after recovery the cache is so polluted with bogus keys that every request triggers an oversized batch query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a thread starvation attack by sending many concurrent requests that all block on the partitioned DB connection pool, exhausting all available threads in the Spring thread pool and preventing legitimate requests from being processed, effectively causing a complete service outage?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause incorrect EVM simulation results by observing that findStorage() returns stale cached values while the actual contract state has been modified by on-chain transactions during the partition, allowing the attacker to predict the stale values and craft eth_call requests that exploit the discrepancy between cached and actual state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a cache inconsistency by sending a request for slot key A of contract X during partition (which fails and leaves EMPTY_VALUE in contractSlotsCache), then after recovery sending a request for slot key B of the same contract, causing findStorageBatch() to include both A and B in the batch query, but the result for A overwrites the EMPTY_VALUE with the correct value only if A exists in DB, otherwise A remains permanently as EMPTY_VALUE?
